extracted_json.title: Porter's Generic Competitive Strategies

blocks.text: According to Porter, for a company to be successful in a particular industry, it must adopt one (and only one) of the following three competitive strategies :

extracted_json.summary_topic_line: Porter's three generic competitive strategies illustrated with the 1980s PC industry.
extracted_json.summary_gist: The page presents Michael Porter's framework of generic competitive strategies, mapped on a matrix of target market (market-wide vs. particular segment) against product (unique product vs. low cost), with examples from the 1980s PC industry (HP, IBM, DELL, Apple). Per Porter, a company must adopt exactly one of three strategies: differentiated strategy, cost leadership, or focus; being 'stuck-in-the-middle' is a fourth, non-recommended outcome.

transcription:
[figure]

(PC Industry in the 1980s)

According to Porter, for a company to be
successful in a particular industry, it
must adopt one (and only one) of the following
three competitive strategies :
   - ① Differentiated strategy : ..... (fill in)
   - ② Cost Leadership : ..... ( " )
   - ③ Focus: .....
   - ④ stuck-in-the-middle (not recommended)
